Based on the code you provided, it seems your `MainLayout` component is correctly passing the `children` as props to your page components. 

If you're still getting the same error message, it's possible that the issue is somewhere else in your codebase. You could try checking if there are any other components that have incorrectly nested `<Routes>` components or if there are any other errors in your code that could be causing the problem.

Additionally, you could try uninstalling and reinstalling the `react-router-dom` package using the following commands in your project directory:

```
npm uninstall react-router-dom
npm install react-router-dom
```

If the problem persists, it could also be helpful to share more of your code or error messages so that we can better understand the issue.

If you're not seeing the RoomMain or RoomDetail components render on the page, it's possible that there is an issue with the URL paths.

Make sure to check that you are visiting the correct URL paths to see each component. In your router file, the RoomMain component can be accessed by visiting `/room`, while the RoomDetail component can be accessed by visiting `/room/:roomId`. If you visit the root URL `http://localhost:3000/` or other paths not defined by the routes, the component will render the Navigate component and redirect to the `trang-chu` path, which may not be what you want.

Another common issue is with the import statements for your components. Make sure that you are importing the correct components into your router file. 

If neither of these solutions fix the issue, please provide more information or code examples so I can help you troubleshoot the issue.

Based on the code you've provided, there doesn't seem to be anything obviously wrong with your Router component. It correctly defines routes for the RoomMain and RoomDetail components, and wraps them with the MainLayout component.

It's possible that the issue is with the RoomMain or RoomDetail components themselves, or with the data they are rendering. 

For example, if the data for the RoomMain or RoomDetail components is not properly fetched, it may prevent the components from rendering on the page. You may also want to check that the components are properly defined and exported.

If the issue persists, try to add some console.log statements in your components to debug the issue, and check the network tab in your browser's developer tools to see if any errors are being thrown when data is being fetched.

Based on the code you've provided, there doesn't seem to be anything wrong with your RoomMain component.

The component defines a search bar and a view change button group that allow users to filter and change the display of room items. It also passes the `roomData`, `searchText`, and `isGridView` as props to the `RoomList` component.

As long as the `roomData` variable contains the expected data and the `RoomList` component is properly defined and receiving the correct props, the component should render correctly.

If you're not seeing the RoomMain component render on the page, make sure that the route to this component is set up correctly in your router file, and that any imported components are properly defined and exported. You can also add some console.log statements to the RoomMain component to help debug any issues.

If the issue persists, please provide more information or code examples so that we can help you troubleshoot the issue.

Based on the code you've provided, there doesn't seem to be anything wrong with your Router component either. It correctly defines the routes for the RoomMain and RoomDetail components.

If the RoomMain and RoomDetail components are not rendering correctly, it's possible that there is an issue with the way they are defined, or with the data they are rendering. 

For example, if the data for the RoomMain or RoomDetail components is not properly fetched, it may prevent the components from rendering on the page. You may also want to check that the components are properly defined and exported.

If the issue persists, try to add some console.log statements in your components to debug the issue, and check the network tab in your browser's developer tools to see if any errors are being thrown when data is being fetched.

Also, make sure that the paths you're using to access the RoomMain and RoomDetail components match the ones in your router config. If the paths are different, the components won't be rendered correctly.
